skill.name: ask-matt
skill.description: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation. A router over the user-invoked skills in this repo.
skill.summary: Documentation router that maps your engineering situation to the right skill in the repo.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: This skill is a pure documentation file with no executable code. All static findings were false positives: backtick-wrapped skill names in Markdown (e.g., `/grill-with-docs`) are formatting, not shell commands. The single URL is a legitimate documentation link. No security risk identified.

content.user_title: Route any engineering situation to the right skill
content.value_statement: When you cannot remember which skill to use, this router maps your situation to the correct flow. It describes the main path from idea to ship and the available on-ramps.
actual_capabilities: Describes the main engineering flow from idea to ship
Identifies on-ramps for common starting situations like bug triage
Explains context hygiene rules for keeping sessions productive
Lists standalone skills for situations outside the main flow
Explains how to cross between sessions using handoff
limitations: Contains no executable code or automation
Does not perform any actions itself - it is a documentation reference
Requires familiarity with the broader skill repository to be useful

use_cases.title: Starting a new project
use_cases.description: Use this router when beginning a new build and unsure which skill to invoke first.

use_cases.title: Triaging incoming issues
use_cases.description: Determine when to use triage versus other flows based on the source of the work.

use_cases.title: Crossing session boundaries
use_cases.description: Learn when to handoff to a new session versus compact the current one.

prompt_templates.title: Basic routing question
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an idea for a new feature. Which skill should I start with?
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new project and unsure where to begin

prompt_templates.title: Triage decision
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a backlog of bug reports. Should I use /triage or go directly to /implement?
prompt_templates.scenario: When deciding how to handle incoming work

prompt_templates.title: Context management
prompt_templates.prompt: My session is getting long. Should I use /handoff or /compact at this point?

prompt_templates.title: Codebase improvement
prompt_templates.prompt: I have spare time between features. What should I run to improve the codebase?
prompt_templates.scenario: When looking for productive downtime tasks

output_examples.input: I have a new feature idea and a codebase. Where do I start?
output: Start with /grill-with-docs to sharpen the idea through interview
It is stateful and retains context in CONTEXT.md and ADRs
Continue in the same session through /to-prd and /to-issues
Each /implement session starts fresh from a single issue

output_examples.input: I have incoming bug reports piling up. What handles those?
output: Use /triage to move issues through triage roles
It produces agent-ready issues for later implementation
Only triage issues you did not create yourself
Issues from /to-issues are already agent-ready - skip triage for those

best_practices: Keep the main flow steps in one unbroken context window until after /to-issues
Use /handoff when you want a fresh session with preserved context
Reach for /compact only at intentional breaks between phases
anti_patterns: Do not compact mid-phase - the agent can lose its way
Do not triage issues that /to-issues already produced
Do not push through degraded reasoning near the smart zone limit

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: It is a documentation router that describes which skill to use for different engineering situations.

faq.question: Does it modify any files?
faq.answer: No. It contains only explanatory text and references to other skills.

faq.question: When should I use /grill-me instead of /grill-with-docs?
faq.answer: Use /grill-me when you have no codebase. It is stateless and saves nothing locally.

faq.question: What is the smart zone?
faq.answer: The window (about 120k tokens) within which the model still reasons sharply.

faq.question: Do I need to run anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Run /setup-matt-pocock-skills before your first engineering flow to configure required settings.

faq.question: Can this skill invoke other skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is user-invoked and only describes which skills to use. You must invoke them yourself.
